body_txt: Let's say you've written something. You know it's good note because you're such a better writer than us , right? but you still want to attract more feedback and compliments than you'd normally get. note Although that's still more than we've ever gotten. So, you go out of your way to call attention to how bad it is note Even though it's nowhere near as bad as this entry , tell everyone why they'd never want to see it, and, in extreme cases, wangst at length about how you're a miserable failure of a writer who deserves to die alone. note What does that makes us by comparison, then? Then, just sit back and watch the sympathy and back-pats roll in. Of course, you can disparage the first ones as insincere to really get them rolling. This is a common tactic of the Shrinking Violet - though in their case, the line between this trope and Chronic Self-Deprecation is often very thin indeed. Subtle Attention Whores also love this trope, as it lets them bask in pity-praise without having to directly toot their own horn. It's also a good strategic move in Passive-Aggressive Kombat and Politeness Judo . This can backfire very badly for the compliment fisher if people respond by agreeing with them. ("It's OK, we love you even though you're a bad writer.") If someone does that on purpose, it's probably a form of Backhanded Compliment . People with particularly bad cases of self-loathing may even prefer this type of response, confirming and mentally justifying their own negative perception of themself. It can also set up a very effective Stealth Insult if the target only objects to one of the putdowns the compliment fisher makes , implying they think all the others are valid points. ("I'm a bad writer, I'm ugly, and nobody likes me!" "That's not true; your writing isn't bad.") A Sub-Trope of Self-Deprecation , and the dishonest, manipulative little sister of the chronic form , though both can result from the same base feeling of self-loathing and can be very hard to distinguish from each other. Compare and contrast Suspiciously Specific Denial . If two people try this at once, it can lead to Outhumbling Each Other . By the way... You don't have to bother editing this page. I know you don't really want to. It's OK, there are much better pages on the wiki for you to spend your time with, I understand if you think this one is a waste... Compare Aren't You Going to Ravish Me? , this trope's creepy, Freudian sister .
